away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: DiJonai Carrington
players.injury_comment: Carrington underwent left foot surgery in April and there's been no indication that she's close to making her season debut.

players.name: Rickea Jackson
players.injury_comment: The season-ending knee injury is a devastating development for Jackson and the Sky, as the 25-year-old forward was well on her way to a career-best campaign. Through Chicago's first four games, Jackson averaged 18.0 points, 4.8 rebounds, 2.0 assists, 1.8 blocks and 1.5 three-pointers in 29.5 minutes per contest. After Jackson's early exit in Sunday's contest, Aicha Coulibaly opened the second half with the starters and looks to be the most likely candidate to move into the top unit for the Sky's next game Wednesday versus the Wings.

players.name: Courtney Vandersloot
players.injury_comment: Vandersloot is still recovering from the right knee surgery she underwent last June to repair a torn ACL, and there's been no indication that she's close to a return.

injuries.name: Courtney Vandersloot
injuries.comment: Vandersloot is still recovering from the right knee surgery she underwent last June to repair a torn ACL, and there's been no indication that she's close to a return.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Alysha Clark
players.injury_comment: The severity of the issue is unclear, though Clark will miss Saturday's contest due to a left leg injury. Her next chance to play will come Monday in Seattle. The veteran forward didn't see the floor in Dallas' last game and logged just five minutes in the prior contest, so her absence shouldn't have a significant impact on the rotation.

players.name: Odyssey Sims
players.injury_comment: Sims exited last Friday's win over the Sparks in a wheelchair and has been ruled out for a third consecutive contest due to a left ankle injury. She is without a clear timetable for a return and can be considered week-to-week until the Wings offer an update on her status.

players.name: Costanza Verona
players.injury_comment: Verona will be inactive as the Wings look to preserve her eligibility as a developmental player. The guard has appeared in just one game this season, logging six minutes against Minnesota on June 9. Her next chance to suit up will come Monday against Seattle.
